paper_title: Pollen analysis of Holocene sediments from the Poço das Antas National Biological Reserve, Silva Jardim, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

methods.description: Process for collecting sedimentary columns from a peaty area between forest fragments for further analysis.

steps.description: Two sedimentary columns were collected in the peaty area between forest fragments of the REBIO.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sedimentary columns from specified locations

steps.description: Hand-excavated holes in the ground facilitated the exposure of sediment profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate holes to expose sediment profiles

steps.description: Aluminum boxes, 40 cm long and 2 cm wide, were used to obtain soil samples.
steps.searchable_summary: use aluminum boxes to collect soil samples

methods.description: Method for determining the ages of sediment samples using radiocarbon dating.

steps.description: Material for dating was collected at specified levels of the sediment columns.
steps.searchable_summary: collect material for radiocarbon dating from specified sediment levels

steps.description: Ages were established through radiocarbon methodology by Beta Analytic Inc., USA.
steps.searchable_summary: send samples to Beta Analytic Inc. for radiocarbon dating

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing pollen samples from sediment columns to determine past vegetation and climate conditions.

steps.description: Three samples from column 1 and two samples from column 2 were selected for palynological analysis.
Chemical treatment followed Ybert et al. (1992). All samples were treated with 10% HCl, 40% HF for a minimum of 12 hours, 40% KOH, C and Zn 2 Cl with density 2.
steps.searchable_summary: chemically treat samples according to Ybert et al. (1992)

steps.description: Exotic spores (Lycopodium clavatum) were added to the samples to calculate palynomorph concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: add exotic spores to samples for concentration calculation

steps.description: Five microscope slides of each sample were prepared.
The calculation of pollen grain percentages was based on total pollen that includes aquatic and hygrophyte taxa, herbs, shrubs and trees, but not algae and spores.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen grain percentages excluding certain taxa

steps.description: Pollen identification relied on the reference slide collection of the Laboratory of Palynology, as well as specialized literature.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen using reference slides and literature

methods.description: Statistical treatment of palynological data using TILIA software to group and plot pollen data.

steps.description: TILIA software was used for statistical treatment of the palynological data.
steps.searchable_summary: use TILIA software for statistical analysis of palynological data

steps.description: Palynomorphs were grouped in two different ways for plotting the pollen data through TILIAGRAPH software.
steps.searchable_summary: plot pollen data using TILIAGRAPH software
